message:
feat(core): add cancellation-safe channel with reserve/commit semantics

Implement a bounded MPMC channel (SafeSender/SafeReceiver) that solves
the silent message-loss problem inherent in standard channel recv():
if a receiving task is cancelled between dequeue and processing, the
message vanishes. This module prevents that with a two-phase protocol:

1. reserve() dequeues the item into a staging area, returning a
   Reservation RAII guard.
2. The consumer inspects (peek) and processes the item, then calls
   commit() to acknowledge.
3. If the Reservation is dropped without commit (e.g. task cancelled),
   the item is automatically returned to the front of the queue for
   the next consumer.

Key design decisions:
- All internal state protected by std::sync::Mutex + Condvar (no async
  runtime dependency, works with any executor)
- SafeSender<T>: Send + Sync + Clone (multiple producers)
- SafeReceiver<T>: Send + Sync + Clone (multiple consumers)
- Reservation<T>: Send (single-owner RAII guard)
- Integrates with CancellationToken via reserve_cancellable() and
  send_cancellable() methods that abort promptly on token firing
- Configurable capacity, max concurrent reservations, and poll interval
- Full metrics tracking (sent, received, returned, timed_out counters)
- Serde-serializable configuration

This is the channel primitive underlying the structured concurrency
scope hierarchy (ft-e34d9.10.3.3).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
